text: Git branching is a powerful feature of version control that helps teams collaborate without conflicts. A typical workflow uses feature branches, where each developer creates a separate branch for a new feature or bug fix. This keeps the main branch stable while work is in progress. When a feature is complete, the developer submits a pull request to propose merging their changes into the main branch. Team members review the code, suggest improvements, and approve the changes. After approval, the branch is merged into the main codebase. This process creates a clear record of changes, reduces errors, and ensures that only reviewed, tested code becomes part of the project.
